feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Manual enrollment of global benefits
entry.content:
Hi,

we have configured pension and insurance benefit plan and has linked to pay components. Employee goes in and manually enrolls to these benefits. Once it is done it the compensation information is not updated with the contribution or allowances like child allowance for which he enrolled manually.#SuccessFactors SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Global Benefits 

Please let me know what could be the issue.

entry.title: ONB: Not able to see the Time management info for Onboardee/inactive Employee
entry.content:
Hi, 

we hired people through ONB, Manage pending hire, they are still inactive employee as their hire date are in the future. but we need to modify the time off data and Time sheet before their arrival.

we modify the As of date in the future (so on their hiring date or later), we go to the Time Management Block and we have  see this (see screen shot 1):

Time Sheet

You are not authorized to access time sheet data or the user does not exist.

Upcoming Time Off

You are not authorized to view time off data.

When we add employee through "add mew employee" and even if their hire date is in the future, we are able to display and modify their Time info. 

we checked the RBP and everything looks ok, so we are not able to fix the issue.

Any advice on this please?

Thanks, 

sevda

entry.title: Workflow email notifications being sent in the wrong language
entry.content:
Hello experts

I am configuring the email notifications so the employees can receive them in Portuguese. Under Email Notifications Template Settings I choose the desire notification (Workflow action Approval Notification, for example) and I make sure that the translation to Portuguese is there. 

Then, with the system in Portuguese, the email notification continues to be sent in English. 

Do you know if I am missing some steps on the configuration or what could be the reason of this situation?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Feasibility of Attaching Document in Mail Notification Alerts in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I hope you are doing well.

One of my customers has this following ask regarding the feasibility of attaching documents to mail notifications triggered by alerts in SuccessFactors. Specifically, I would like to understand whether it is possible to include actual document attachments in these email alerts or if only document links can be embedded in the notification. Could you please confirm if attaching documents is supported, and if so, provide any relevant guidelines or configurations to enable this feature? Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Employment Info/ Event Reason Audit Report
entry.content:
Hi there, 

I have converted most Canvas and Table reports into Story reports, in anticipation for the complete switch over to Story reports. 

It is disappointing to find that Story Reports do not allow me to make employment info audit reports. We regularly monitor event reason specifically to ensue the correct even reasons are being chosen. Thankfully, the Table report is reliable and gives me the audit information. 

When can we expect to see Story reports to be able to run audit reports ? 

Thanks 
Aisling

entry.title: Restricting visibility of Pay Components in "Add New Employee" page
entry.content:
Hi Community,

 

In the "Add New Employee" page, both recurring and non-recurring pay components are appearing in the recurring pay components dropdown under the Compensation Information section. This might be causing confusion for users when selecting the appropriate pay component type.

 

Is there any way so that non-recurring pay components will get displayed separately from recurring ones?

 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

entry.title: DRTM Master data Purge
entry.content:
We tried purging inactive user via DRTM Master data Purge. The approver did not receive any approval in Purge Request monitor and purge progress and results - it directly gave result as "completed with empty report"
Request your support as this is on priority

Thanks
SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: A Deep Dive into Role-Based Permissions: Best Practices for Access Management
entry.content:
What are Role-based Permissions?

Role-based permissions are a security model in SAP SuccessFactors that allows you to grant or restrict the system access to any user.

It helps organizations ensure that the right people are getting the right permissions in the system.

Role based permissions has three main elements called:-

  • Permission Groups
  • Permission Roles
  • Target Population
  1. Permission Groups – Group of Employees

Permission groups are the user or user’s who share similar permissions or certain attributes.

  1. Permission Roles – Set of permissions

Permission roles are set of permissions a user can access or do in the system.

  1. Target Population – Target population are employees for whom behalf the action has been performed. It is denoted by t.

Note:- All permissions doesn’t required the target population, only some permissions requires that and it’s denoted by t at the end of the permission.

 

You can identify the permission groups, roles and target population by finding out who, What and whom in your requirements. Where Who represents the permission groups, what represents the permission roles and whom respresents the target population.

 

To simplify the steps, you can divide it into below steps:-

  1. Permission Groups
  2. Permission Roles
  3. Granted Population
  4. Targeted Population
  5. Assign permissions to the Granted Population
  6. Define who are the targeted Population

Process to implement the RBP is as below:-

Permission Groups -> Permission Roles -> Granting Permission Roles to Permission Groups - > Target Population

Let’s try to understand the role-based permissions with example. Suppose a customer wants that only HR managers can create/update or delete the objectives/goals for employees in US.

 

Here HR manager in USA will be our Granted population.

Create/Update or delete the goals and other permissions are permission roles.

All employees who work in USA are the targeted population.

 

Procedure to Implement RBP

Pre-requisites-

You must have access to role based permission.

 

  1. Permission Groups

To create Permission Groups, User must have relevant Permission.

In Demo Environment, I logged in as Aanya Singh(sfadmin){Superadmin for the system}

Procedure:-

In Homepage, under search for actions or people search for Manage Permission Groups -> Create New - > Give a unique name -> Select the employees you want to Grouped -> Done.

 

Give a Name to your Group Name-> Select the employee whom you want to give permission.

In below example:-  We have grouped Human resource employees who are working in US and excluded the employees who job level is Executive Support.

Group Name :: Granted_User_Sugandha

User Type :: Employee

 

 

Result:-

Now we have successfully created the group Granted_user_Sugandha under permission groups, you can see we have 23 Active memberships.

 

 

  1. Permission Roles

To create Permission Groups, User must have relevant Permission.

In Demo Environment, I logged in as Aanya Singh(sfadmin){Superadmin for the system}

Procedure:-

Go to Manage Permission Roles -> Create - > Basic information -> Add Permission -> Done.

 

Img 2.1                                                                              Img 2.1

 

The next step is to fill in Basic information in Create Role such as Name and description(optional). In the example below, we have named Human Resource_US (Sugandha_Test) -> Click Next.

                                                                           Img2.2

Add Permissions, Give the required permission you want to give to the user. We have  User Permission and Administrative Permission.

After adding the required permission, save it.

 

  1. Granting Permission Roles to Groups

Go to Permission roles -> Under Human Resource_US_Test -> Add Role Assignment -> Basic Information - > 

 

 Whom you want to give access, to all employees or from Groups, If Group than Which group you want to give access, select the group & if you want to add their direct managers you can do and Save.

 

  1. Target Population

Note- We have given additional permission in the roles for human resource_US as shown in below image, Under User permissions, Object plan permission need a Target Population (Denoted by t symbol), Where we need to add Target population. In below example Human resource_US can create a goal for employees in US., our target population is employees in USA.

entry.title: Is it possible to integrate two different SuccessFactors instances? Not in same landscape!
entry.content:
Hello Dear Members,

I would need your inputs on this question.

My customer has a requirement to integrate two different SuccessFactors landscapes, where data for one legal entity needs to be transferred from the Parent Instance to the Child Instance via SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) or any other possible way.

  • Is this integration technically feasible?
  • If yes, what challenges might we face during implementation?
  • If not, are there any recommended workarounds?

Additionally, can the integration include all data (e.g., employee master data, payroll-related information, organizational structure, etc.), or is it limited to transferring only a Mini Master Data (such as Employee Profile)?

Looking forward to insights from the community on best practices or possible limitations for such an integration.

Best regards,

Siva

entry.title: Base pay amount Pay component always on top of list of pay component in compensation in UI
entry.content:
Hi All,

In the User visual/UI of compensation information portlet, is it possible to keep the base pay amount- Pay component always on top/as a first line of listed paycomponents?

 "Base pay amount" pay component remains first when the new paycomponent is inserted using Insert record (History icon - Insert New record) . But when it's done through a WF approval(pencil icon (edit)), then the new paycomponent visible as first line in compensation part not a basw pay amount in first. 

So, is there any possibility to keep "Base pay amount" pay component always on first line/top among of all other pay components in compensation information portlet whether we do insert new record or edit (via approval)?

Thank you!

entry.title: It is now possible to sync Time Off Leave in SF with MS Teams Calendar. Can same be done in Slack?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

As of this release it is possible to sync Time Off status between SAP SF and MS Teams (works both ways).
Do you know if similar can be done with Slack tool as well? For SAP SF to send Time Off leave information to Slack and update status there as well for Out of office?

Thanks in advance!

Djurdja

entry.title: Business rule to trigger a custom email template based on Job info attributes
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I am trying to configure a rule which triggers a workflow that is designed to send the manager of an individual a custom email template.

The rule looks at the probationary end date and if today is -28 days from it, it triggers the workflow.

Since the removal of the basic rule type, i cant seem to find the right scenario to base the rule on.

I have tried the generate alerts rule type, and whilst that does trigger the workflow and trigger an alert, it does not send the custom email template assigned to the workflow at all.

attached are snips of the rule and the workflow currently.

Thanks!email

entry.title: Setup Guide for Manage Promotion process
entry.content: We are looking for setup guides on how to setup and structure the forms and workflow for the manage promotion process within Successfactors?  All guides i have found do not provide detailed steps but only test scripts for once this is setup.

entry.title: Employee agreements in EC
entry.content:
Currently, when my customer transfers employees in EC or updates employee pay conditions, they need to re-issue a new contract. They use Spinifex Strato for initial contract generation in SAP Recruiting. To re-generate a contract, they create a Job Requisition and move the internal employee through the Recruiting process.

It seems that to streamline this process, we may need an additional Strato solution for contract generation directly in EC. However, I believe other companies have faced similar challenges and might have identified more efficient ways to generate employee agreements within EC.

Are there potential options for generating employee agreements in EC?

entry.title: How to save/export business rules to PDF - truncates long lines
entry.content:
Hi there,

I'm trying to save a business rule to a PDF, but many of the statements are truncated when I try to print.

e.g. part of the rule looks like this:

But when I try to print / save to PDF (in landscape), it cuts off the longer statements so they can't be seen:

Does anyone know of a clever way to print/save this, without having to individually screenshot each page?

Many thanks,
Alan

entry.title: Update Parent fields depending on child fields forma MDF Object
entry.content:
Good afternoon!

We have two MDF objects with a Parent-Child relation. We need to modify a field of the parent object depending on the values ​​assigned in the child object at the time of saving the data.

However, when generating a rule with a base object “MDF Child” we cannot see the fields of the parent in the then clause.

If we generate a rule with a base object “MDF Parent” it is not executed at the time of saving if the fields of the parent object are not modified.

Is there a way to carry out this process through rules?

Can the permission to edit the parameters be assigned as with the Basic rules?

entry.title: Not able to find certification exam for C_THR81_2405
entry.content:
Not able to find certification exam for C_THR81_2405 under learning.sap.com>get certifications

Also, selecting any certification by clicking on 'Schedule Exam' is redirecting me "Buy Learning Hub" which I did not used to see earlier.

entry.title: Hide Payment End date for some pay component and for some company code
entry.content:
Hi All,

We want to hide "payment end date (Define end date)" for some pay component and some company code in compensation portlet. There are 3 pay components with "End-Dated Payments" = Yes, and many countries use them.
We want to hide the "payment end date " field for the only two company codes.
We tried to create a rule, but it doesn't work properly. 

So, could you please suggest is it possible to hide "payment end date" for some company code and some pay components using business rule or any other solutions? PFA screenshot for reference

Thank you

entry.title: Intelligent Service Center with Global Assignment
entry.content:
If we want to use Intelligent Service Center to trigger an integration when an event : 

- add Global Assignment

- End Global Assignment

where need to be added the business rules : 

SAP_EVENT_ENDGA

SAP_EVENT_ADDGA

these prepackaged business rule are using entity Jobinformation 

(cf https://help.sap.com/doc/e3eafa2654d84564a039e8bcb6218ea0/2411/en-US/SF_IS_SettingUp_Impl.pdf)

there is no details where we need to add the business rule.

we have added a post-save business rule in job information under Manager Business Configuration but the number of Global Assignments in Intelligent Service Center > Add Global Assignment remains to zero

even after creating a new Global assignment

 

we don't know if the problem is on the business rules or on manage business configuration.

if we need to add the business rule elsewhere, please let us know.

entry.title: HRIS Sync Mapping Guidelines
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues,

In HRIS Sync, is it acceptable to map multiple standard fields to a single HRIS field? So that a single HRIS field data is transmitted to multiple standard fields. In the below example, considering the emplStatus is already hardcoded, can we have below custom mapping to a custom field. I think yes.

Also are there any limitations that prevent mapping one standard element and one user info element together? For example, below: 

I have referred to a few SAP notes and help documentation, but the statements are often contradicting.
